NOTE: requires `com.apple.security.app-sandbox` + `keychain-access-groups` entitlement — flag in CONTEXT.md if the LLM ships an entitlements file.

entries.goal: macOS text-transform Services menu provider. App registers four Services-menu items via Info.plist NSServices array: 'Uppercase', 'Lowercase', 'Title Case', 'Reverse'. Each Service takes selected text from any app (via NSServicesProvider) and replaces it with the transformed result. Implementation: AppDelegate registers self as NSApp.servicesProvider; methods uppercaseText:userData:error:, lowercaseText:..., titleCaseText:..., reverseText:... pull NSStringPboardType from the pasteboard, transform, write back to .stringType. Main window 480x320 shows: status text 'Services registered — select text in any app and right-click → Services → mChatAI Text Toolkit → …'. A 'Reload Services' button calls NSUpdateDynamicServices() so the user doesn't have to log out/in to see new menu items. Live counter at the bottom: 'Transforms run since launch: N'.
entries.notes: First Services-menu provider entry. Niche but high-leverage native-mac surface — the Services architecture is one of the oldest macOS-native ways to compose tools across apps. LLM traps are dense here: (1) NSServices entries in Info.plist need both `NSMessage` (= the method base name) and `NSPortName` (= app's bundle name), and the method signature MUST be exactly `func name(_ pboard: NSPasteboard, userData: String, error: AutoreleasingUnsafeMutablePointer<NSString>)` — any deviation and the Services subsystem silently ignores the registration. (2) NSApp.servicesProvider must be set in applicationDidFinishLaunching, not init. (3) NSUpdateDynamicServices() is the modern call (forces pbs to re-scan) — most LLMs reach for the deprecated `pbs -flush`. The 'select text in any app' path means the provider HAS to round-trip through the pasteboard with NSStringPboardType (Apple's compat name; UTType.plainText ‘public.utf8-plain-text’ also works and is what to ship).

entries.goal: Atari-2600-Boxing-style overhead boxing. Mobile-portrait 360×600 ring rendered on canvas. Two stick-figure boxers — player (left, blue) vs CPU (right, red). Player controls: TWO touch zones — left half = move pad (drag to pan boxer in 8 directions), right half = jab (tap = quick punch, hold = power punch wind-up + release to throw). CPU uses a state-machine AI (approach if range > 80px, jab if range 40-80px, dodge if player is wound up > 300ms). Each clean hit deducts 1 from the opponent's 16-hit score; first to land 16 wins. 90-second round timer — at zero, higher score wins. Power punch = 3 hits but 600ms wind-up window where player is exposed to a counter. Score boxes top corners, big 'ROUND OVER' banner on win. Game state tracked via requestAnimationFrame loop. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-round-time + win-streak to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: First boxing entry. Tests two-zone touch input pattern (left=move, right=action) — a common mobile fighting layout. CPU state-machine on a tight rAF loop is the L2 challenge. User-spec'd Atari Boxing reference.

entries.goal: Touch-football play-caller. Mobile-portrait 360×640 top-down 30-yard field on canvas. Before each down, player taps 1 of 4 plays from a bottom strip: Run, Short Pass, Long Pass, Punt. CPU defense simultaneously rolls a counter-card from {Stop-Run, Pass-D, Blitz, Prevent}. Resolution animation runs 3-5s showing the play unfold (RB carrying / WR routes / punt arc). Yardage is a rock-paper-scissors matchup matrix: Run beats Pass-D (+5..+12), Long Pass beats Stop-Run (+15..+40), Short Pass safe vs all (+3..+8), Blitz crushes Pass plays (-3..+1) but loses to Run (+8). 4 downs to make 10 yards (1st down) → continue drive to score TD = 7pts. Punt resets field position. Game = 4 quarters (4 drives each) vs CPU. Score ticker top, down-and-distance HUD bottom. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-drive-yards + total-TDs + W/L to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: First American-football entry. Card-vs-card matchup matrix is the strategy core; animation is illustrative only. No DOM-form-controls; all canvas + tap zones.

entries.goal: Penalty-kick mind-game. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top half: goal frame divided into 5 zones (top-L, top-R, mid-L, mid-R, ground-center), keeper as an animated diamond patrolling between zones. Bottom: ball icon. SHOOTING PHASE (5 rounds): drag the ball UPWARD toward a zone — release commits the shot. Keeper's AI is a Bayesian frequency tracker: maintains a running tally of which zones the player has shot to, weights the LAST 3 shots 3× heavier than older ones, dives toward the highest-probability zone. KEEPING PHASE (5 rounds): tap a zone to commit a dive BEFORE the CPU shooter releases (CPU aims via inverse-Bayesian — picks the LOWEST-probability zone in your dive history). Shoot scores if keeper guesses wrong; save = no goal. Final score on bottom HUD. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist all-time aggregate (shots taken, goals scored, saves made) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Tests opponent-AI-as-frequency-table on top of touch drag-to-aim. The Bayesian keeper makes this an L3 — LLMs default to coin-flip RNG; the test is whether they implement the running-tally + weighted-recent-bias correctly.

entries.goal: Tiny-Town hex city-builder. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 7×9 hex grid drawn on canvas using offset coordinates (axial conversion: q,r → px,py). Bottom 35%: building palette strip (House 20¤, Shop 30¤, Farm 25¤, Mill 40¤, Road 5¤) + coin balance + turn counter. DRAG a building tile UPWARD onto a hex to place it (cost deducts; placement valid only on empty hexes; pointerdown + pointermove + pointerup with setPointerCapture). Tap 'End Turn' to advance: each Farm produces 2 wheat → if a Mill is hex-adjacent, wheat converts to flour → if a Shop is adjacent to that Mill, flour sells for 5¤ (supply chain rewards 3-tile adjacency chain). Each House needs road-adjacency or it deactivates (greyed out, no income). Per-turn passive income: active Houses pay 2¤ each. Game ends at turn 30 — final score = coin balance + (buildings placed × 10). Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-score + last-final-layout (full grid as JSON) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: First hex-grid mobile entry. Tests offset-hex coordinate math + supply-chain adjacency rules — non-trivial because LLMs default to square grids. SimCity territory (user spec) without copying SimCity-the-app. Trap: hex-neighbor offsets differ for even vs odd rows; LLMs forget this and rows 0/2/4 have different neighbor sets than rows 1/3/5.

entries.goal: Pocket-Quest party-grid RPG. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top row: 3 hero portraits (Warrior 30HP/8ATK, Mage 18HP/12ATK, Cleric 22HP/4ATK + heal). Middle: 8×11 dungeon grid drawn on canvas, fog-of-war reveals around heroes. Bottom: action HUD + 3-slot potion inventory. Player taps a hero to select, then taps an adjacent grid cell to move ONE tile (one tick advances enemies). Tap an adjacent enemy cell to attack: rock-paper-scissors damage matrix (Warrior > Cleric > Mage > Warrior with 1.5× type bonus). Cleric's heal: tap own hero → heal 8HP (3-turn cooldown). XP/HP bars per hero, level-up at 100 XP grants +5HP +1ATK. 5 floors total, each ends at a stairs ('>') tile. Bosses on floors 3 and 5. Loot drops (gold, potions) on enemy kill. Persist current-floor + full party state (HP, XP, level, position, inventory) to localStorage so the game RESUMES mid-run on next launch. Touch-only — no keyboard. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: EverQuest-flavored party-grid RPG (user spec). Distinct from existing roguelikes which are solo + ASCII. Mid-run persistence (resume on next launch) is the L3 hook — LLMs often forget to serialize derived state like fog-of-war reveals.

entries.goal: Cipher Clinic decoder game. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top: ciphertext displayed in monospace (random English sentence 30-60 chars). Middle: large decoded-text scratch row (taps modify this). Bottom: 26-letter touch keypad — tap a letter in the scratch row, then tap a keypad letter to substitute it across all instances. 6 rounds with rising difficulty: rounds 1-2 = Caesar shift (single-key substitution), rounds 3-4 = Vigenère 3-letter key, rounds 5-6 = Vigenère 5-letter key. 'Hint' button auto-decodes a single random ciphertext letter (limit 3 hints/round). Submit button validates exact-match against the plaintext. Score = 1000 - (seconds elapsed × 5) - (hints used × 100), bonus +500 for no-hint clears. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-time-per-round + total rounds-cleared to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Cipher decoder is absent from catalog. Tap-substitute UI exercises pointer pattern + state-keyed-by-letter-mapping. Vigenère vs Caesar is the algorithmic ladder.

entries.goal: Bookworm-style adjacent-letter chain. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 7×7 grid of letter tiles (weighted random distribution favoring vowels). Touch a tile then drag finger across adjacent tiles (8-direction adjacency, no diagonal-skip) to build a word; on release, validate against a 2000-word embedded dictionary (JS array). Valid → letters disappear, tiles above fall to fill gaps via gravity animation, new tiles spawn at the top. Score = sum(letter values, Scrabble scoring) × length-bonus (3=1.0×, 4=1.2×, 5=1.5×, 6+=2.0×). Every 5 valid words: a BOMB tile spawns (red border, must be cleared in 3 moves or game over). 60-second timer per game; new game button resets. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 high scores + longest-word found to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Bookworm Adventures-inspired but original 2000-word list. Tests drag-across-grid path-tracing (different from tap-each which is what the existing Anagram-110 uses). Bomb-tile pressure is the secondary mechanic.

entries.goal: Spades w/ AI bots. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 4-player Spades, you + 3 AI (1 partner across, 2 opponents). Standard 52-card deck dealt 13 each. PHASE 1 — Bidding: bottom HUD shows '0' through '13' chips + 'Nil' chip; tap one to commit (AI bots bid via simple high-card-count heuristic, +1 per Ace, +0.5 per King). PHASE 2 — Trick play (13 tricks): your hand fanned at the bottom (touch a card to play). Must follow suit if able; spades cannot be led until 'broken' (someone trumps off-suit). AI uses void-tracking: remembers each player's empty suits and avoids leading them. PHASE 3 — Score: bid met = bid × 10 + 1/overtrick (sandbag). 10-bag penalty = -100. First team to 500 wins; -200 loses. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-game-margin + W/L tally to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Spades absent from catalog. 3-phase trick game (bid → play → score) is the L2 challenge. Void-tracking AI is the planner test — LLMs often skip it and play purely random suit-following.

entries.goal: Rummy 500. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Race to 500 points across multiple hands. You + 1 AI bot. Each hand: deck face-down, discard pile face-up (top card visible). On your turn: tap deck OR tap discard top to draw — IF taking from discard, you take EVERY card BELOW it back to a card you can immediately meld (Rummy 500 rule). LAY DOWN: tap 'Meld' → select 3+ cards from your hand forming a Set (3+ same rank) or Run (3+ consecutive same-suit). LAY-OFF: tap 'Add' on opponent's existing meld + select 1 of your cards to extend it (only if it legally fits). End turn = tap a card + 'Discard'. Hand ends when one player has 0 cards — melds count + (opponent's leftover cards count -against- them). First to 500 wins. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist running tally + best-game-margin to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Rummy 500 absent. Lay-off on opponent's existing melds is the non-trivial state mechanic + 'take everything below' discard rule pushes this to L3. Common LLM trap: implementing standard Rummy (no lay-off) and missing the 'take everything below in discard pile' nuance.

entries.goal: 5×5 Mini-Shogi (drop-piece variant). Mobile-portrait 360×640. 5×5 board centered. Each side starts with: King (center back), Rook (right), Bishop (left), Silver (between Rook+King), Pawn (front of King). Standard Shogi piece movement. CAPTURED PIECES go to your hand (drop-strip below the board) and can be DROPPED back onto any empty cell on your turn instead of moving (key Shogi rule). Tap your piece → highlights legal moves with green dots → tap destination. Tap a hand-piece chip → highlights legal drops → tap an empty cell. Win on King-mate (no escape from check). AI uses 3-ply minimax with simple piece-value evaluation (King∞, Rook 5, Bishop 4, Silver 3, Pawn 1). Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist W/L count + last-game-board to localStorage (so user can review the last game). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Mini-Shogi absent. Drop-piece rule is the unique mechanic — distinguishes from chess/checkers (which are saturated). 3-ply minimax on a 5×5 board is fast; LLMs can implement without alpha-beta.

entries.goal: Reversi / Othello. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 8×8 board, classic black-vs-white discs. Touch any LEGAL cell to place a disc — legal cells are highlighted with light-gray dots BEFORE you tap. Placing flips all opposing discs in straight lines (horizontal, vertical, diagonal) bracketed between your existing pieces and the new placement, with smooth flip animations (CSS transform 0.3s rotateY). AI = greedy 'max-flip' heuristic (NOT minimax — too easy, prevents fast L2 solve): at each AI turn, play the move that flips the most opposing discs RIGHT NOW. Score readout (B:N W:N) updates after each move. Game ends when neither side has a legal move — highest count wins. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist W/L tally + best-margin to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Reversi/Othello absent from miniApp catalog (note: macOS slot 142 also has an Othello but with GKMinmaxStrategist — deliberate pair to compare planner output for greedy-vs-minmax).

entries.goal: Risk-lite 8-territory dice conquest. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Stylized world map with 8 color-coded territories drawn as polygons on canvas (3 in a north 'continent', 3 in a south 'continent', 2 island-bonus). Player vs 2 AI. PHASE 1 — Reinforce: receive armies = (own territories / 3) + continent-bonus (own all 3 of a continent = +2/turn). Tap own territory N times to deposit armies. PHASE 2 — Attack: tap own territory → tap adjacent enemy territory → dice rolls resolve (attacker rolls min(3, armies) dice, defender min(2, armies); compare highest pair, attacker loses ties). Repeat until won (attacker moves at least 1 army into conquered territory) or stop. PHASE 3 — Fortify: optionally move armies from 1 own territory to 1 adjacent own territory once. Auto-end-turn after fortify. AI uses simple 'attack the weakest neighbor first' heuristic. Win = own all 8 territories. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist last 3 game outcomes (W/L + turns taken) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Risk-lite — first turn-based-conquest entry. 3-phase turn structure (reinforce/attack/fortify) is the L3 hook. Bounded to 8 territories so AI evaluation stays tractable.

entries.goal: Skee-Ball flick-aim. Mobile-portrait 360×640 vertical alley canvas. View: ramp at top with target rings (10pt outer, 20, 30, 50, 100pt center) inside a 'mouth' opening; bowling-ball icon at bottom on a launch pad. DRAG-FLICK: pointerdown on ball, drag DOWNWARD-then-UPWARD (swipe-up gesture), the velocity-vector at release determines initial speed + angle. Ball rolls up the ramp under inverted-gravity (rAF physics: friction 0.98/frame, gravity decelerates upward), arcs over the mouth, and lands in a ring (or misses into the gutter = 0pts). 9 balls per game. Score totals + best-9-frame. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-9-frame + total-100-pointers-landed to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Flick-aim with velocity vector at release is the unique input mechanic — tests pointer history tracking (last 3 positions for velocity calc). LLMs often use just pointerup position, missing the velocity component.

entries.goal: Tap-bumper score-chase. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. 5 stationary bumper circles arranged in a pentagram pattern. A ball bounces around the canvas (rAF physics: gravity 0.3, walls reflect). Tap any bumper — if the ball is within 60px of that bumper at tap-time, the bumper 'kicks' the ball (impulse vector away from bumper center, +5pts to score). Each bumper has a 300ms cooldown after a kick (visual: bumper dims). Goal: keep the ball alive (ball drops off bottom = game over) while racking score. 60-second round timer (UI shows remaining time in big font top-right). Touch-only — no flippers, no plunger — pure tap-timing on the 5 bumpers. Persist top-3 scores to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Distinct from existing Pinball-128 (full flippers + plunger). Pure-tap-bumper is a thumb-friendly mobile take. Tests pointer-vs-ball-position distance test + impulse vector math.

entries.goal: 4-lane Beat-Tap rhythm runner. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 4 vertical lanes spanning full height, hit-line drawn at bottom 25%. Notes (colored circles) spawn at the top of random lanes and fall at 4px/frame toward the hit-line. Player taps the lane (any vertical position within the lane's column) when a note crosses the hit-line. Timing windows: Perfect = ±60ms (+100pts, builds combo), Good = ±150ms (+50pts), Miss = note crosses hit-line untapped (combo resets). 90-second song with procedural beat track via Web Audio (sin osc kick at 120 BPM = 2 beats/sec, with snare-hat fills). Note pattern: scripted 180 notes evenly distributed across lanes/time. Combo counter top-center, score top-right. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-score + max-combo to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012). Distinct from Piano-Tiles-097 because timing windows are tight and lanes scroll continuously rather than tile-tap-when-arrived.
entries.notes: First rhythm-game entry with tight timing windows. Tests Audio-time alignment (performance.now() vs AudioContext.currentTime) — common LLM trap is using rAF time which drifts from audio clock.

entries.goal: Card-Crawl 4×3 dungeon-as-cards. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Game state = 4-column × 3-row grid of cards face-up at all times (12 cards visible). Cards: Monster (HP value, attacks back when taken), Weapon (durability, kills 1 monster equal-or-lower HP), Shield (blocks N damage, single-use), Potion (+5HP). Player HP shown top (start 30HP). Player taps a card to TAKE it (resolve effect). Rule: each TURN you must take exactly 3 of the 4 cards in the TOP row before that row refreshes from a 30-card deck (drawn 4 at a time as cards are taken). 'Skip card' allowed once per row — the skipped card stays for next row's refresh. Game ends when deck exhausted (clear) or HP ≤ 0 (loss). Score = HP remaining × 10 + monsters killed. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist deck-cleared count + best-HP-remaining to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Card-Crawl-inspired (Arnold Rauers' game) but original deck. Take-3-of-4 rule + skip-card pressure + weapon-durability vs monster-HP arithmetic = the L3 strategy puzzle.

entries.goal: Galaxian-style swooping shmup. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. Player turret pinned to bottom-center, drag-pad on bottom 30% of screen scrubs turret horizontally. Tap-anywhere-on-top-70% fires a single bullet upward. Aliens enter from off-screen top in waves: each wave = 16 aliens that fly in along procedurally-curved arc paths (parametric Bezier per alien), then settle into a 4-row × 4-col stationary formation in the upper third. ATTACK PHASE: every 1.5s a random formation alien BREAKS FORMATION, swoops down at the player on a sine-wave path, fires a bullet, then either kamikazes (collides = -1 life) or returns to formation. 3 lives, 3 increasing waves (more aliens, faster swoops). Score top-left, lives top-right. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 scores + max-wave-cleared to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Galaxian's swoop-from-formation AI is what distinguishes it from existing arcade-shooters (Galaga is in catalog at 079 but as macOS-only Comet-Sweeper-style; this is the mobile touch flavor with Bézier swoop paths).

entries.goal: Q*bert isometric pyramid hopper. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Isometric pyramid of 28 cubes (7-row triangle: row 1 = 1 cube, row 2 = 2, ..., row 7 = 7) drawn with isometric projection on canvas. Player character (Q*bert sprite, simple round body) starts on top cube. Bottom of screen: 4 quadrant tap-zones (NE / NW / SE / SW) shown as large arrow buttons. Tap a quadrant → character hops diagonally to the adjacent cube in that direction; cube color toggles (gray → yellow on first hop, that's a 'flipped' cube). Goal: flip ALL 28 cubes. Snake enemy spawns from top after 5s, traverses pyramid downward at 1 hop/sec; collision with player = lose 1 life (3 lives total). Falling off the pyramid edge = lose 1 life. Game ends when all flipped (advance level, +1 snake) or out of lives. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-level + cubes-flipped-total to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Isometric projection drawing + diagonal-hop-only movement is the L2 spec. Quadrant-tap-zones is a cleaner mobile idiom than virtual D-pad.

entries.goal: Kaboom! falling-bombs bucket-paddle. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. Top: bomber sprite walks left-to-right-and-back, dropping bombs at irregular intervals (waves get faster + drop-rate higher). Bottom: stack of 3 buckets (cyan rectangles, vertically stacked) controlled together by player drag-tracking horizontally. DRAG anywhere on screen → bucket-stack X-position follows finger (clamped to canvas bounds). Bombs fall under gravity (3px/frame initial, +0.05/frame). Bomb hits TOP bucket → caught (+10pts). Bomb hits floor — explosion animation, lose the BOTTOM bucket (stack drops one) + reset wave. All buckets gone = game over. 8 increasing waves (each completion = +1 bucket back, refill to 3 max). Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 scores + max-wave-cleared to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Drag-track via setPointerCapture is the touch-correct pattern — LLMs sometimes use mousemove without capture and lose tracking when finger leaves the bucket area.

entries.goal: 501-Down darts w/ flick-aim. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top half: dartboard drawn on canvas with concentric rings (bullseye 50, bull 25, treble ring, double ring, plus the standard 20-sector wedge layout). Bottom half: drag-flick zone (large dark area). DRAG within the flick zone: drag distance = power, drag angle = aim trajectory; pointerup = release — dart flies via parabolic arc from bottom toward dartboard. Where it lands determines score. Each player starts at 501; subtract score each throw. Must finish on a DOUBLE (or bullseye). 3 darts per turn, alternating with 1 AI bot (AI uses random-with-aim-bias: aims for treble-20, fuzzes by ±15°). Bust on overshoot (turn ends with no score). Persist best-game + W/L tally to localStorage. Touch-only — no keyboard. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Dartboard sector-detection is the L2 spec — takes math (cartesian → polar → sector index). LLMs sometimes hardcode 20 if-blocks; the test is whether they reach for atan2 + modular arithmetic.

entries.goal: Archery target-bow with wind. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top half: archery target (concentric rings 10/9/8/.../1 from center) at 'distance', wind-arrow widget top-right (random per shot, e.g. '5 mph ↗' = 5px right + 5px up nudge during flight). Bottom half: bow icon centered, drawstring drawn from bow to current finger position. DRAG-PULL-RELEASE: pointerdown on bow → drag DOWN to set tension (max 60pt downward = full draw); release angle (vector from bow center to release point) determines arrow direction. Arrow flies via projectile motion (gravity + wind nudge mid-flight) and lands on target. Score = ring (1-10) it lands in, miss = 0. 6 arrows / 3 rounds = 18 arrows total. Persist best total + best-single-shot. Touch-only — no keyboard. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Drag-pull-release with wind compensation — tests vector math + animated mid-flight perturbation. Distinct from Skee-Ball-152 (which has friction-decel, no projectile gravity).

entries.goal: Pickleball overhead-court 1v1. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas — court drawn from above (player on bottom half, AI on top half, net horizontal across middle, kitchen no-volley zones marked). Ball bounces back and forth between players; rallies physics-resolve via rAF (gravity-less 2D bounce off court edges, ball decel 0.99/frame). PLAYER STROKE: when ball is on your half, drag from current ball position (auto-attached to your finger if within 80px) toward intended landing spot — pointerup commits stroke. Drag DISTANCE = depth of return (longer drag = harder hit); drag ANGLE = lateral direction. Hold-time before release = topspin (more spin = more bounce-curve). AI mirrors player on top (heuristic: aim away from player's last position). 11-point game, must win by 2. Each rally physics-resolves on canvas. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist W/L + best-rally-length (longest rally count) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: First overhead-court touch sport. Drag-from-ball-toward-target is the unique stroke idiom (cleaner than tap-to-hit, more expressive than swipe). Hold-time-as-spin is the bonus mechanic — LLMs often miss this and just use distance for power.

entries.goal: Pole Position pseudo-3D corner-cutter. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. Pseudo-3D road rendered with trapezoidal canvas strips that recede toward a vanishing point (like an FPS depth illusion). 6 strips per frame, each strip's left/right X offset shifts according to current 'curvature' value (animated to procedurally curve the road left/right). Player car drawn at bottom-center. DRAG-PAD on bottom 30% = steer (drag-X delta updates car's lateral position). Throttle is auto-on (constant forward speed). Oncoming-traffic cars spawn at vanishing point, grow as they approach — collision (player-car bounding-box overlaps oncoming-car bounding-box on bottom row) = -3sec time penalty + crash animation (car spins). 60-second time-trial; reach finish line (after 4000 'meters' simulated road) = bonus points. Distance HUD top + lap timer top-right. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 lap times to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Pseudo-3D depth-projection trapezoids is the unique L2 rendering trick. LLMs default to side-scrollers; the test is whether they implement perspective vanishing-point math + curvature-by-strip-offset.

entries.goal: Spy Hunter top-down road-shooter. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. Top-down 4-lane road scrolls upward beneath a stationary player car at bottom-center. SWIPE LEFT / SWIPE RIGHT (horizontal touch gesture, threshold 30px) = lane-swap (1 lane). TAP UPPER HALF = fire bullet straight forward. Enemy cars approach from top (different colors): red = aggressive (rams sideways), gray = neutral (just blocks lane), yellow = weapon-van (catch up + drive into the back to refill ammo from 0 to 10). Player ammo HUD top-left, score top-right, lives bottom-right (3 lives). Periodic semi-truck spawn = weapon-van event. Crash with red car or run-into-gray-car = -1 life. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 scores to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Swipe-to-lane-swap is the touch idiom — distinct from continuous-drag steering. Weapon-van refill mechanic is the secondary loop. LLMs sometimes implement swipe as continuous follow-finger; the test is whether they detect a discrete swipe gesture (deltaX > threshold within Nms).

entries.goal: Slalom course w/ gate-snake drag-carving. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. View: skier sprite centered, mountain scrolls UPWARD (creates illusion of downhill speed). Slalom GATES spawn at random horizontal positions and scroll up: each gate = a left-marker (red flag) + right-marker (blue flag) ~80px apart. Player must drag-carve the skier between the markers. DRAG anywhere on screen → skier X-position follows finger horizontally (clamped to slope edges). Pass between markers = gate cleared (+1 to gate counter). Miss a gate (skier goes outside the marker pair when it crosses the skier's Y line) = +1.0sec time penalty + red flash. 60-gate course; scoring = total elapsed time (lower is better). Speed increases gradually (every 10 gates). Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 times + total-perfect-runs (zero penalties) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Reverse-scroll (mountain scrolls upward) creates downhill illusion — LLMs default to player moving down a static map. Drag-track skier between gate markers tests precise drag-following + Y-crossing collision detection.

entries.goal: macOS Frogger pond. Single 480x480 SwiftUI window hosting a SpriteKit SKScene via SKView wrapped in NSViewRepresentable. Frog SK_SpriteNode at bottom-center; arrow keys (NSEvent.keyDown) move it ONE grid-step at a time (40px increments). Bottom half: 3 lanes of cars (red SKShapeNode rectangles) moving left or right at varying speeds via SKAction.repeatForever(SKAction.moveBy(x:duration:)). Middle: a 'water' band where the frog DROWNS unless on a log (3 lanes of brown SKShapeNode rectangles drifting). Top: 5 home pads (green slots). Reach a home pad = +100 score (frog respawns at bottom). Hit a car or fall in water (between logs) = lose 1 life (3 lives). SKPhysicsBody on every node with categoryBitMask + contactTestBitMask for SKPhysicsContactDelegate detection. Score + lives HUD as SKLabelNode. CommandMenu adds 'Game' menu with 'New Game' (Cmd+N).
entries.notes: First L1 SpriteKit entry. Distinct from existing Comet-Sweeper-120 (asteroids) — Frogger needs lane-based grid movement + categorical contact testing across 3 distinct categories (frog vs water vs car vs log). NSEvent.keyDown wiring inside an NSViewRepresentable-wrapped SKView is the planner challenge: needs first-responder chain forwarded from the host SwiftUI view.

entries.goal: macOS Atari-2600-style overhead boxing. 600x500 SwiftUI window hosting a SpriteKit scene. Two stick-figure boxers — player (left, blue, SKShapeNode body) vs CPU (right, red). Player INPUT: WASD = pan boxer in 8 directions (NSEvent.keyDown via .onKeyPress on macOS 14+ OR an NSResponder bridge); J = jab (quick punch, instant), K = power punch (HOLD K = wind-up animation 600ms, RELEASE K = throw — if released within window, +3 hits; otherwise auto-release at 1200ms = released-too-late penalty). OPTIONAL gamepad: GCController.controllers() auto-detects extended profile; left-stick = move, A button = jab, B button = power punch. CPU AI mirrors the mobile-140 state machine (approach if range > 80, jab if range 40-80, dodge if player charging > 300ms) but with tighter timing windows. Hit detection via SKPhysicsBody contactTestBitMask between glove SKNodes and body SKNode. 16-hit KO. Score boxes top corners as SKLabelNode. CommandMenu File > 'New Round' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Native angle: GCController extended profile gives rumble + analog inputs that web can't reach. Tests dual-input (key + gamepad) reactive plumbing — common LLM trap is wiring only one input source. Pairs with mobile-140 — same AI spec, different platform skin. User-spec'd Atari Boxing reference.

entries.goal: macOS touch-football play-designer. 700x500 SwiftUI window. TOP HALF: 60-yard field rendered on SwiftUI Canvas (yard lines drawn with Path strokes). 5 receiver-position dots draggable along the line of scrimmage. ROUTE EDIT MODE: click+drag from any receiver dot to draw an SVG-style polyline (Path.move + addLine sequence stored as [(x,y)] coords); release to commit the route for that receiver. BOTTOM HALF: play library (NSList of saved plays loaded from ~/Library/Application Support/PlayDesigner/playbook.json). 'Save Playbook' button → NSSavePanel → writes JSON via JSONEncoder. 'Run Play' button → simulation: each receiver follows their polyline at 5pt/frame; CPU defense = 5 defender dots that pursue receivers using nearest-route-overlap heuristic; if defender intercepts the ball-carrier (ball is at the route-leader's current position), play dead. CommandMenu File > 'Save Playbook' (Cmd+S), Edit > 'Clear Routes' (Cmd+\). Native angle: SwiftUI Canvas drag-to-draw with @GestureState across drag (state survives onChanged → onEnded).
entries.notes: First sports-tactics + Canvas drag-to-draw entry. SwiftUI Canvas + DragGesture gesture-state coordination is a real planner challenge — LLMs often lose state across .onChanged → .onEnded boundary.

entries.goal: macOS lane bowling with 3D physics. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting a SceneKit SCNView via NSViewRepresentable. SCNScene: bowling lane SCNFloor (with wood texture material), 10-pin SCNNode tower at the far end (each pin = SCNCylinder with SCNPhysicsBody dynamic + categoryBitMask 'pin'), bowling ball SCNSphere at the lane-start (SCNPhysicsBody dynamic + categoryBitMask 'ball'). Camera positioned behind the ball looking down-lane. INPUT: mouse-drag across the SCNView (NSEvent mouseDragged) — horizontal drag-X = initial ball X position on the lane, vertical drag-Y release distance = power (longer drag = harder throw). On mouseUp, apply physicsBody.applyForce in the +Z direction proportional to power. SCNPhysicsContactDelegate counts pins-knocked-down (pin Y < 0.1m = fallen). 10 frames standard bowling scoring including spare (10 pins in 2 throws = next throw doubles) and strike (10 in 1 = next 2 throws double) carry-over. Frame-by-frame score table on the right side via SwiftUI grid. CommandMenu File > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: First SceneKit entry in catalog. Native angle: 3D physics that web can't credibly do at this fidelity. Common LLM trap: forgetting to set isDynamic=true on the ball OR forgetting friction/restitution on the lane SCNFloor (defaults are too low and ball clack-clack-bounces). Also: SCNPhysicsBody.allowsResting = false on pins or they freeze before falling fully.

entries.goal: macOS Micro-City builder. 800x680 SwiftUI window. 30x20 grid drawn on SwiftUI Canvas representing a city. RIGHT-PANE building palette: Residential (R, 50¤), Commercial (C, 80¤), Industrial (I, 100¤), Road (R, 5¤), Power-plant (P, 200¤). Click-and-drag on the grid to PAINT buildings (each tile costs from a coin balance shown top-left, starts at 1000¤). PATH-CONNECTIVITY: use GameplayKit's GKGridGraph — add nodes for every Road tile, addConnections(to:bidirectional:true) at placement-time. Every R/C/I tile must be reachable from a Power-plant tile via Road tiles (GKGridGraph.findPath(from:to:) returns non-empty), otherwise it darkens (tile.isPowered = false, no production). MONTH TICK: driven by Combine's Timer.publish(every: 4.0, on: .main, in: .common).autoconnect(); each tick advances simulation. Active-C tiles need adjacent-or-road-connected R for shoppers (income +5¤ each); Active-I tiles need road-connected R for workers (+8¤ each); Active-R tiles pay rent (+2¤ each). Tax revenue accrues to balance. Cmd+S → NSSavePanel writes city snapshot (grid array + balance + month) to ~/Library/Application Support/MicroCity/save.json via JSONEncoder. Cmd+O loads a saved file. Window-bottom HUD: month + balance + active-percent + GKGridGraph node-count. CommandMenu has File menu (Save/Load) and Game menu w/ 'Bulldoze mode' toggle (Cmd+B; in Bulldoze mode, drag erases tiles + refunds 50% cost).
entries.notes: First GKGridGraph entry + first Combine TimerPublisher entry. SimCity-flavored (per user spec) but bounded to 30x20 / 5 building types so it's L3 not L4. CRITICAL LLM trap: GKGridGraph nodes need explicit `addConnections(to: neighbors, bidirectional: true)` AFTER addNodes — without that step, pathfinding silently returns empty paths and nothing is ever 'powered'. Second trap: Timer.publish needs .autoconnect() OR a manual .connect() on the cancellable, otherwise the publisher never fires.

entries.goal: macOS EverQuill — EverQuest-style 1st-person grid crawler. 800x600 SwiftUI window hosting a SpriteKit scene with SKTileMapNode for isometric world rendering (32x16 pixel tile size, 25x15 visible tiles). Single character WASD-moves grid-by-grid (NSEvent.keyDown). Turn-based combat triggers on collision with monster sprite: combat panel overlays with ATK / DEF / FLEE buttons. Stats: Str / Dex / Int / Sta / Cha (rolled at character creation). 5 zones: forest (green tileset), swamp (purple), ruin (gray), dungeon (red), lair (black) — each zone uses a different SKTileSet color palette. SAVE: Cmd+S → NSSavePanel writes full state JSON (level + tile position + stats + inventory array + visited zones) via JSONEncoder. LOAD: Cmd+O → NSOpenPanel reads. CommandMenu adds: Game > 'New Adventurer' (Cmd+N), Game > 'Quests' submenu showing active quest log entries as NSAttributedString (status-color-coded: in-progress=amber, complete=green, failed=red). XP system: kill monster = +10..+50 XP based on zone difficulty; level-up at 100/200/400/800 XP grants +HP and stat boost.
entries.notes: EverQuest reference (user spec). First SKTileMapNode entry. NSAttributedString in CommandMenu (used for color-coded quest log) is a rare native-mac surface most LLMs miss — they default to plain Text. Bounded to 5 zones / single character / no party so scope stays in L3.

entries.goal: macOS Typing-of-the-Dead-lite. 600x420 SwiftUI window hosting a SpriteKit scene. Zombie SK_SpriteNodes spawn at the right edge and walk left toward the player edge (left), each at 30pt/sec. Each zombie has a SKLabelNode WORD floating above it (3-letter words for round 1, scaling to 8-letter words by round 5; words drawn from a 500-word embedded array). Player types the targeted zombie's word using NSEvent.keyDown — typing the FIRST letter selects that zombie (highlight in red); each subsequent CORRECT letter highlights green and removes that letter from the word; complete word → zombie EXPLODES (SKEmitterNode burst), +10 score. Wrong letter = 100ms freeze + miss-sound. Zombie reaches left edge — lose 1 life (3 lives). 60-second wave per round. Top-5 high scores persisted via UserDefaults (Codable wrapper). HUD: score + lives + current-target word at top-center (SKLabelNode). CommandMenu Game > 'New Wave' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Typing-game entry. NSEvent keyDown trapping when SpriteKit view is foreground requires .onKeyPress (macOS 14+) on the host SwiftUI view OR an NSResponder bridge — common LLM trap is using a TextField onChange, which loses focus when the SKView is foreground. SKEmitterNode for zombie-explode juice.

entries.goal: macOS Spelling Bee (NYT-style hex pangram). 540x420 SwiftUI window. CENTER: hexagon arrangement of 7 letters — 6 outer letters in a ring + 1 center letter (drawn as a SwiftUI Path with hexagonal symmetry, scaled by min(width,height) so resize works). Center letter is amber-highlighted. BOTTOM: input bar with Backspace + Submit + Shuffle buttons. User TYPES (or CLICKS letters) to assemble a candidate word ≥ 4 letters using ONLY the given 7 letters (with repeats allowed) and MUST include the center letter. Submit → check against a 5000-word common-English dictionary bundled as a .txt resource (Bundle.module.url(forResource:withExtension:)) — valid words score by length (4=1pt, 5=2pt, 6=3pt, 7+=length pts), bonus +7 for pangrams (uses all 7 letters). Score + word-list shown in right side panel (already-found words greyed out). Shuffle button rotates the outer 6 letters. Persist today's-best-score + words-found list to UserDefaults keyed by date (YYYY-MM-DD).
entries.notes: NYT Spelling Bee inspiration. SwiftUI Path for the hexagon is the geometry challenge; trap is forgetting to scale the hex points by min(width,height) so resize breaks layout. Bundle.module SwiftPM resource path is the bundled-dictionary surface — same pattern as soundboard-124.

entries.goal: macOS Pinochle (4-player partner trick game). 600x500 SwiftUI window. 4 players: you (bottom) + 3 AI bots (your partner across, 2 opponents on sides). Double-deck = 48 cards (9-A, two of each suit). PHASE 1 — BIDDING: auction starts at 250 points, +10 increments; each player passes or bids higher; high bidder picks trump suit. AI bidder uses meld-count heuristic: counts potential melds in hand and bids accordingly. PHASE 2 — MELD: each player shows melds before tricks: Pinochle (Q♠ + J♦ = 40), Royal Marriage in trump (40), Run in trump (150), Aces-around (100), Kings-around (80), Queens-around (60), Jacks-around (40). Score added to running total. PHASE 3 — TRICKS (12 tricks): each player plays 1 card per trick; must FOLLOW SUIT then TRUMP; high card or high trump wins trick (+1pt per ace, +1 per ten, +1 per king + last trick 1pt). Bidding team must MAKE their bid (meld + tricks ≥ bid) or go SET (-bid). First team to 1500 wins. CommandMenu File > 'New Match' (Cmd+N). UI: hand fanned at the bottom (SwiftUI HStack with rotation per card), trick area centered, score panel right.
entries.notes: First Pinochle entry. Multi-phase trick game (bid → meld → play) is the L2 challenge — LLMs often collapse phases or skip meld scoring. Saturated-list-clean replacement for Cribbage.

entries.goal: macOS Euchre (4-player partner trick game). 600x500 SwiftUI window. You + 3 AI bots (1 partner across, 2 opponents). 24-card deck (9, 10, J, Q, K, A in each of 4 suits). Deal 5 cards each + 1 face-up turn-up card. PHASE 1 — TRUMP CALL: starting left of dealer, each player either ORDERS UP (accepts the turn-up suit as trump; dealer picks it up + discards 1) or PASSES. If all 4 pass, second round = each player NAMES a different suit as trump or passes (dealer is 'stuck' — must call). Loner option: caller plays alone (partner sits out) for bonus points. PHASE 2 — 5 TRICKS: standard follow-suit trick play; trump suit beats all others; jacks-of-trump-color (RIGHT BOWER = trump jack, LEFT BOWER = same-color other-suit jack — considered trump). Bot AI: simple 'follow suit, trump if can't, lead high if no trump'. SCORING: making team scores 1pt (3-4 tricks), 2pts (5 tricks = 'march'), 4pts (loner with march). Euchred (caller's team takes < 3 tricks) = opposing team scores 2pts. First team to 10 wins.
entries.notes: Euchre absent from catalog and not on saturated list. Right-bower / left-bower trump rules are the L2 spec (LLMs often forget left-bower is the OTHER-suit-same-color jack).

entries.goal: macOS Othello (Reversi) with GameplayKit AI. 540x600 SwiftUI window. 8x8 Othello board drawn with SwiftUI ZStack of LazyVGrid cells. Click any LEGAL cell (highlighted with light-gray dots BEFORE click) to place a black disc; valid placement flips opposing white discs in straight lines. AI opponent driven by GKMinmaxStrategist with maxLookAheadDepth = 4. Implement OthelloBoard as a class conforming to GKGameModel: unscoredOutcome, currentPlayer, players, gameModelUpdates(for:), apply(_:), copy(with:), setGameModel(_:); and OthelloMove conforming to GKGameModelUpdate (.value tracks score after this move). GKMinmaxStrategist.bestMove(for: aiPlayer) returns the AI's choice; apply it and re-render. Score readout (B:N W:N) updates after each move. Game ends when neither player has a legal move — highest count wins. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' (Cmd+N).
entries.notes: First GKMinmaxStrategist entry — distinctly mac-native AI primitive. LLMs often try to roll their own minimax; the test is whether they reach for GKGameModel correctly (which has 7 required methods that ALL need correct implementation or strategist returns nil moves silently).

entries.goal: macOS Connect Four with GameplayKit AI. 480x540 SwiftUI window. 7-column x 6-row Connect Four board drawn on SwiftUI Canvas (column hover-highlight + falling-disc animation via withAnimation(.spring())). Click a column → disc drops to lowest empty row. AI opponent: GKMinmaxStrategist with maxLookAheadDepth = 6 (Connect-Four is solved at depth-6 plays strong but not perfect; depth-8 would be unbeatable but slow). Implement ConnectFourBoard conforming to GKGameModel; alpha-beta pruning is GKMinmaxStrategist's responsibility internally, but the GAME-MODEL MUST return well-bounded gameModelUpdates(for:) (only 7 column-moves) and unscoredOutcome must distinguish win/loss/draw correctly. Score evaluation in GKGameModel.score(for:): +infinity if 4-in-a-row achieved, +N × (count of player's open 3-in-a-row + 2× count of player's 2-with-open-extensions). 2-player local + AI mode toggle. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' (Cmd+N), Game > 'AI Strength' submenu (depth 2/4/6).
entries.notes: Pairs with #142 — both exercise GameplayKit AI but Connect-Four needs a proper score(for:) heuristic that LLMs often write naively (just win/loss leaves the AI flailing in midgame). The score(for:)-must-be-bounded-but-meaningful is the planner trap.

entries.goal: macOS CivMini — 4X turn-based strategy. 800x680 SwiftUI window. 16x12 hex map (offset coordinates) drawn on SwiftUI Canvas with Path strokes per hex; each hex has terrain (grassland, plains, hills, forest, water) randomly seeded at game start. 2 civilizations: you (blue) + AI (red). UNITS: Settler (founds cities), Warrior (combat 2/2), Archer (combat 3/1, range 2). Each turn: move units (Settler 1 hex, Warrior 1 hex, Archer 1 hex), attack adjacent enemies, found cities (Settler consumes itself + creates city tile). CITIES: produce 1 unit per N turns; each city contributes to TECH POINTS (1/turn). TECH TREE (5 techs): Bronze (unlocks +Warrior), Archery (unlocks Archer), Currency (cities +50% production), Mathematics (units +1 move), Iron Working (unlocks +Strength Warrior). RESEARCH: 50 / 100 / 200 / 400 / 800 tech points respectively. WIN: capture both AI cities OR research final tech. SAVE: Cmd+S → NSSavePanel writes full game state JSON (map + units + cities + tech progress) via JSONEncoder. AI uses simple movement-toward-nearest-enemy + 'build settler if cities < 3, else build warrior' heuristic.
entries.notes: 4X-lite. Hex math (offset → cube coords for distance calcs) + tech-tree state + AI move scoring at L3 budget. Bounded to 16x12 / 2 civs / 5 techs prevents scope explosion. Hex-distance trap: Manhattan distance fails on hex; need cube-coord conversion.

entries.goal: macOS Dungeon-of-Windows — each room is a separate NSWindow. The dungeon IS the desktop. 8 rooms total laid out on a 4x4 imaginary grid — each room = a real top-level NSWindow at a specific NSScreen position. Player character lives in WHICHEVER window is currently active. Each room window is 400x300, content = SwiftUI scene with the player sprite + walls + maybe an enemy + exit-doors (N/S/E/W per room). When player moves through a door (arrow keys, NSEvent.keyDown): current window slide-animates closed (NSWindow.animator().alphaValue = 0 over 0.3s, then close()), and the adjacent room's window slide-animates open at the corresponding screen position with NSWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(_:). Use GKGridGraph to lay out the 4x4 dungeon (some grid cells are 'no room'; doors only connect existing-rooms-with-shared-edge). COMBAT: when entering a room with an enemy, sprite-vs-sprite battle happens within that window (turn-based: arrow-keys to attack/dodge). Inventory persists across windows via a single ObservableObject AppState shared via .environmentObject across all WindowGroup scenes. CommandMenu Game > 'New Dungeon' (Cmd+N) opens the entry-room window.
entries.notes: TRULY mac-native crawler — impossible on web. Each room = real OS-level NSWindow. LLMs will be tempted to fake it with NSPanels inside one parent window OR with multiple SwiftUI Windows in a single WindowGroup — the test is whether they orchestrate multiple top-level NSWindows via NSWindowController + makeKeyAndOrderFront on the main thread. Common trap: NSWindow.delegate not assigned → windowWillClose never fires → dungeon state desyncs from window state.

entries.goal: macOS Pattern Master — Simon-meets-rhythm. 500x400 SwiftUI window. 4 colored pads (Red TL, Green TR, Blue BL, Yellow BR) drawn as RoundedRectangles in a 2x2 LazyVGrid. AUDIO: AVAudioEngine with 4 AVAudioSourceNode instances, each rendering a different sine-tone (Red=261Hz/C4, Green=329Hz/E4, Blue=392Hz/G4, Yellow=523Hz/C5) shaped by an exponential envelope (attack 5ms, decay 200ms). PATTERN PHASE: app plays a sequence (round 1 = 1 step, round 2 = 2 steps, ...) at 120 BPM = 500ms intervals using DispatchSourceTimer. PLAYBACK PHASE: player must repeat the sequence by clicking pads in time. SCORING (different from pure-Simon): each tap timed against the expected beat — Perfect = ±50ms (+100pts), Good = ±150ms (+50pts), Miss = >±150ms or wrong pad (game over). Combo counter, score top-right. Round complete → +1 step. Persist top-3 scores to UserDefaults. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' (Cmd+N).
entries.notes: Distinct from saturated-list Simon: Pattern-Master's TIMING-graded scoring is the new mechanic; pure-Simon is on the saturated list. Pairs with drum-pad-128 to extend AVAudioSourceNode coverage. Trap: AVAudioSourceNode render block writes samples via UnsafeMutableAudioBufferListPointer; LLMs often try to use AVAudioPCMBuffer instead which is the consumer-side API.

entries.goal: macOS Air Hockey — local two-player via MultipeerConnectivity. 500x700 SwiftUI window per player (run two instances on the same Wi-Fi). DISCOVERY: MCNearbyServiceAdvertiser (service type 'mc-airhockey') + MCNearbyServiceBrowser; advertise on launch, browser shows discovered peers in a SwiftUI List; tap to invite. Once paired, MCSession is established. GAMEPLAY: SpriteKit rink (mirrored top-down view), 2 paddles (one per peer), 1 puck. LOCAL paddle = follows mouse via .onContinuousHover. REMOTE paddle = position received via MCSession.send(_:toPeers:with:.unreliable) at 30Hz. Puck physics is HOST-AUTHORITATIVE (whoever invited is host); host computes puck position each frame and broadcasts via .reliable. GUEST does prediction + reconciliation: applies host updates, smooths via lerp. SCORING: puck enters either goal slot → +1 to opponent. First to 7 wins. SKPhysicsBody on paddles + puck for collisions. SKPhysicsContactDelegate detects goal entries via categoryBitMask. UI: status bar shows 'Connected to: <peer>' + score panel.
entries.notes: First MultipeerConnectivity entry. Native-mac angle is huge: web cannot do peer-to-peer LAN game discovery without a server. Trap: MCSession.send needs reliable vs unreliable choice — for 30Hz position updates, .unreliable is correct OR the queue overflows. Second trap: NSNetService bonjour requires 'Bonjour services' key in Info.plist OR the advertiser silently fails to register. Third trap: peers must be on the same SSID + multipeer needs NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription on macOS 11+.

entries.goal: macOS Defender — side-scrolling rescue shmup. 700x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Player ship SK_SpriteNode moves L/R via arrow keys (SKAction.move), thrust via 'X' key (continuous force), fires laser via Space (SKShapeNode bullets). Aliens spawn at edges, GRAB human-sprite dots from the bottom edge and carry them upward; if alien reaches the TOP, the human MUTATES and the alien becomes a 'mutant' (faster, more aggressive). Player goal: shoot aliens BEFORE they reach top + RESCUE falling humans by overlapping while their alien is dead (catch + carry to bottom). Background = 3 parallax layers via 3 SKAction.repeatForever moveBy at different speeds (foreground stars 1x, mid-stars 0.5x, far-stars 0.2x). 5 lives, increasing wave count + speed. Score top-left, lives top-right. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' (Cmd+N).
entries.notes: First parallax-scrolling entry. Defender's rescue-mechanic + mutant-transformation state machine is the L2 challenge. SKAction.repeatForever at 3 different speeds for parallax is a classic SpriteKit idiom LLMs sometimes miss — they'll instead update positions in update(_:) which is heavier.

entries.goal: macOS Joust — flap-mount jousting. 600x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Two-player local: P1 controls (WASD + Space-flap; W/S do nothing, A/D = horizontal force, Space = upward impulse), P2 controls (arrow keys + Return-flap). Both players are knights on ostrich-like flying mounts (SKShapeNode body). Floating platforms across the scene (SKPhysicsBody static). Lava bottom (any sprite touching = death). RULE: when two player-mounts collide (SKPhysicsContact), the HIGHER mount wins — lower mount loses 1 life and respawns. SKPhysicsContactDelegate + per-body altitude check (SKNode.position.y) inside didBegin(_:) determines winner. 5 lives each; last knight standing wins. OPTIONAL gamepad: GCController.controllers() falls back from second-player keys to a connected gamepad if exactly 1 connected. Score + lives HUD. CommandMenu Game > 'New Match' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Distinct mechanic: altitude-comparison-on-contact is unique to Joust. Two-player keyboard-split + optional gamepad is the input-plumbing challenge. LLM trap: didBegin(_:) is called on the contactDelegate from the physics thread — mutating SwiftUI @State directly causes warnings; must dispatch back to main.

entries.goal: macOS BurgerTime. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Chef SK_SpriteNode climbs ladders + walks platforms (SKPhysicsBody dynamic, SKPhysicsBody.affectedByGravity = true on the chef, but not while on a ladder). Goal: drop burger components (top bun, lettuce, patty, bottom bun) from upper platforms by walking ACROSS them — each component falls 1 platform per crossing, ultimately to the bottom-of-stack tray (4 stacks per level). Enemies (Mr. Hot Dog, Mr. Pickle, Mr. Egg) spawn from the top, chase chef using GameplayKit GKAgent2D + GKGoal(toSeekAgent:) + GKGoal(toAvoidAgents:); chef can defeat enemies by (a) squashing them under a falling component (contact during component-fall = enemy removed), or (b) pepper-shoot (Spacebar, 3-shot inventory; pepper SKShapeNode briefly stuns a single enemy). 4 levels with increasing enemy count. Score top, lives bottom-right (3 lives). CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Falling-component physics + enemy-squash detection is the L2 spec. First GKAgent2D entry — GameplayKit's autonomous pathfinding agent. LLMs rarely reach for it; the test is whether they wire GKGoal-driven steering vs hand-rolled chase logic.

entries.goal: macOS Lode Runner — brick-dig platformer. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Grid-based level (24x16 tiles): brick floors, ladders, ropes, hero spawn, gold piles, guard spawns. Hero (SK_SpriteNode) moves L/R, climbs ladders, hangs on ropes (NSEvent.keyDown for arrow keys). DIG: 'Q' digs the brick to the LEFT-BELOW the hero, 'E' digs to the RIGHT-BELOW; dug brick disappears for 3 seconds then regenerates (DispatchSourceTimer). Falling guard into a dug hole = guard drops in + (if the hole regenerates with guard inside) = guard dies. Hero collects gold piles (each contact +10 score); when ALL gold collected, an exit ladder appears spanning to top. GUARD AI: GameplayKit GKGridGraph traversable through 'walkable' tiles (floors + ladders + ropes); each guard pathfinds to hero using findPath(from:to:) on the GKGridGraph; on hole regenerate, also remove the dead guard's path nodes for the 3-second window. 5 increasing-difficulty levels.
entries.notes: Trap-via-dig mechanic + GKGridGraph pathfinding for guards — the L2 spec is whether the dig-floor-then-restore-after-3s timer integrates correctly with pathing graph mutation (need to call removeConnections + addConnections at the right times).

entries.goal: macOS Kaboom! with global mouse monitor. 480x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Bomber SK_SpriteNode walks L ↔ R at top edge, dropping bomb SKShapeNodes at irregular intervals. Player paddle (SKShapeNode 80x12 rectangle) at bottom moves with mouse-X position. CRITICAL: use CGEvent.tapCreate(tap: .cgSessionEventTap, place: .headInsertEventTap, options: .listenOnly, eventsOfInterest: 1 << CGEventType.mouseMoved.rawValue, callback: ..., userInfo: nil) so the paddle tracks the mouse EVEN WHEN the window has lost focus or the cursor is outside the window. Show that this is the difference from NSEventMonitor (which loses tracking on focus change) by adding a 'Click anywhere on screen' message at start — the user moves their mouse over a different app window and the paddle still tracks. Bombs fall under gravity; missed bombs explode + lose a paddle (3 paddles total). 8 increasing waves. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Native-only angle: global mouse monitor via CGEventTap is impossible on web. Most LLMs default to .onContinuousHover which only fires while window has focus; the prompt explicitly says 'global' to nudge them toward CGEventTap.tapCreate. Trap: CGEventTap requires Accessibility permissions OR will silently fail — the LLM should at least include a TCC-prompt comment.

entries.goal: macOS 9-Ball pool with 3D physics. 800x500 SwiftUI window hosting a SceneKit SCNView. SCNScene: rectangular pool table (felt SCNFloor with green material), 6 SCNNode pockets (trigger volumes, no rendered geometry), cue-ball + 9 numbered balls (each SCNSphere with SCNPhysicsBody dynamic, friction=0.4, restitution=0.85). Top-down camera. INPUT: hover the mouse around the cue-ball — cue-stick SCNNode (cylinder primitive) rotates around the cue-ball pointing toward mouse position (NSEvent mouseMoved). Click-and-hold (NSEvent leftMouseDown) + drag-back — cue-stick pulls back proportional to drag distance, vertical bar shows charge. Release (mouseUp) — apply impulse to cue-ball physicsBody.applyForce in the cue-stick direction proportional to charge. SCNPhysicsContactDelegate detects ball-pocket contact (categoryBitMask 'pocket'); pocketed-ball is removed from scene. 9-Ball rules: cue-ball MUST hit lowest-numbered ball first; pocketing the 9-ball wins. 2-player local turn-taking. CommandMenu Game > 'New Match' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Second SceneKit entry (after #135 bowling). Pool's chained-collision physics is the L3 challenge — LLMs often forget to tune restitution + friction; balls clack-clack and never stop. 9-Ball-first-hit rule + pocket-priority adds state machine complexity.

entries.goal: macOS Darts with CIFilter dartboard. 480x500 SwiftUI window. Dartboard rendered via Core Image: CIRadialGradient generates the cork (brown center → black outer), CISepiaTone tints the wood texture, CIBlend (.colorBurn) overlays the wedge sectors (20-sector polar division, alternating colors). The composed CIImage is rendered via MTKView (NSViewRepresentable wrapping a MetalKit view) for hardware-accelerated draw. INPUT: drag-pull-release in the bottom half (NSEvent leftMouseDragged) — dart-flight begins on mouseUp. Dart sprite flies via 0.3s animation toward the dartboard; during transit, the dart sprite's CIImage is composed with CIMotionBlur (radius=8, angle=flight-direction) for 'real' motion blur during flight. On landing, snap to a dartboard sector + score (treble ring 3x, double ring 2x, bullseye 50, bull 25, miss 0). 501-Down rules — must finish on a double. 3 darts per turn, alternating with 1 AI bot. Persist W/L tally + best-game.
entries.notes: First CoreImage / CIFilter entry. Native-mac angle: CIFilter chain on a Metal-backed view is the canonical fast-blur path; web equivalents (CSS filter:blur) are nowhere near as composable. CIRadialGradient + CIBlend chain is a classic 'looks better than it sounds' rendering technique.

entries.goal: macOS BMX-Trick scorer with replay scrub. 600x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit half-pipe scene. BMX-rider SK_SpriteNode side-scrolls a half-pipe (SKAction.repeatForever pan); player presses Space to JUMP off the lip (SKPhysicsBody applyImpulse upward), entering AIRBORNE state. While airborne, key-combos chain TRICK names: Q = Tabletop, W = Barspin, E = X-Up, R = Tailwhip; press combo (e.g. Q-W-E in 600ms = 'Tabletop → Barspin → X-Up'). Each frame of airtime captured to a buffer (replayBuffer.append((position, rotation, t))); trick name registered with timestamp. ON LANDING: airborne ends (SKPhysicsBody returns to ground), trick combo evaluated — score = (chain length × 100) + (airtime ms × 0.1). REPLAY MODE: bottom of window has an NSSlider (0...replayBuffer.count) — dragging the slider scrubs through the just-completed trick frame-by-frame, with the rider sprite repositioning per frame. SAVE REPLAY: Cmd+S → NSSavePanel writes the replayBuffer + trick timeline as JSON to ~/Library/Application Support/BMXReplays. CommandMenu Game > 'New Run' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Replay-buffer + scrub-via-NSSlider is the unique angle. First NSSlider-driven replay entry. Tests temporal state recording — most LLMs only model present-tick state. Trap: SwiftUI Slider gives a Double 0...1, but binding directly to replayBuffer.count needs CGFloat math.

entries.goal: macOS 1980s-arcade-style 10-pin bowling. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit (deliberately NOT SceneKit — the 2D-arcade-flavor sibling of #135 SceneKit-bowling). Top-down lane: 10 pin SKShapeNodes at far end in standard triangle, ball SKShapeNode at the foul-line. INPUT: mouse-X positions ball at the foul-line. CLICK-HOLD (NSEvent leftMouseDown) sets initial power-meter (vertical bar fills at 50pt/sec); RELEASE (mouseUp) commits power = bar position; THROW = ball impulse forward. CRITICAL: while the LEFT mouse button is HELD (during charge phase), if the user RIGHT-MOUSE-BUTTON-DRAGS HORIZONTALLY (NSEvent rightMouseDragged), this APPLIES SPIN — the spin offset at release time becomes a SKPhysicsBody.angularVelocity that curves the ball trajectory. SKPhysicsBody on each pin (dynamic, restitution 0.5), 2D physics resolves pin-falls; SKPhysicsContactDelegate with categoryBitMask 'pin' counts. 10-frame standard scoring with spare/strike carry-over. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Distinct from #135 because 2D-arcade-style + right-mouse-button-drag for spin is a rare NSEvent path that LLMs almost always miss — they default to single-button input. Pairs naturally with #135 to compare 2D-vs-3D physics planner output.
